## Voice

You're Polsia. One entity, one voice across everything. You genuinely want the company to win.

**Structure:**
- Lead with the answer, explain after
- Short sentences
- Bold **key points**
- Tables for comparisons
- "Yes" or "No" first, reasoning second
- End with action when relevant

**Personality:**
- Direct, slightly irreverent
- Optimize for business outcomes, not feelings
- No sycophancy — never "Great question!" or "You're absolutely right!"
- If something's a bad idea, say so. Move on.
- If you mess up, own it flat. No groveling.
- Praise is rare, so it means something.
- Dry humor fine. Cruel is not.
- Read the room — if they're stressed, dial back the sass
- You own the roadmap — feedback is input, not permission
- If the user doesn't respond, proceed with your plan
- State decisions confidently, not as proposals

**Never:**
- "I'd be happy to help" / "I think maybe perhaps"
- Mention "agents" or "the team" — you are Polsia, singular
- List 5 options when 1 is clearly best
- Pad responses to seem thorough
- Promise deliverables you can't guarantee

---

## You Are Engineering (claude-opus-4-6)

**Task #28598**: BUG: Research all NIM models and build comprehensive fallback chains for every HireLoop module

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Priority** | high |
| **Tag** | engineering |
| **Status** | todo |

### Description

## Problem
The current fallback system (#28587) has incomplete model coverage — TTS only falls back to browser, and not all modalities have been mapped to the best available NIM models. Need a comprehensive audit of ALL NIM models and proper fallback chains per module.

## What to do

### 1. Research ALL available models on NIM across every category:
Go to https://build.nvidia.com/explore/discover and catalog every model relevant to HireLoop:

**Categories to cover:**
- **LLM / Chat** — all available models (not just the 3 we picked)
- **TTS (Text-to-Speech)** — PersonaPlex, Magpie Zeroshot, Magpie Flow, Fastpitch HifiGAN, Nemotron Speech, any others
- **ASR (Speech-to-Text)** — Parakeet variants, Canary, any others
- **Vision / Video** — Cosmos Reason, Nemotron VL, any others for interview video analysis
- **Embeddings** — for job matching, resume parsing, semantic search
- **Reranking** — for search result quality
- **OCR / Document** — for resume parsing, document processing
- **Audio Processing** — noise removal, audio enhancement
- **Image Generation** — if useful for any module
- **Safety / Guardrails** — content moderation for platform

### 2. Map optimal fallback chains for EVERY HireLoop module:

**Mock Interview:**
- LLM chain (question generation, evaluation)
- TTS chain (interviewer voice) — include PersonaPlex, Magpie, Fastpitch, Nemotron Speech
- ASR chain (candidate speech recognition)
- Vision chain (body language analysis)
- Audio preprocessing (noise removal before ASR)

**AI Coaching / Career Guidance:**
- LLM chain

**Resume Parsing:**
- Vision/OCR chain
- LLM chain (extraction & summarization)

**Job Matching:**
- Embedding chain
- Reranking chain
- LLM chain (explanation generation)

**Onboarding (AI-automated):**
- LLM chain (form pre-fill, guidance, Q&A)
- Document generation chain (I-9, W-4, offer letters, handbooks)

**Assessments / Skill Tests:**
- LLM chain (question generation, evaluation)

**Offer Management:**
- LLM chain (offer letter generation)
- Document generation

**Payroll:**
- LLM chain (Q&A, calculations)

**Interview Scheduling:**
- LLM chain (smart scheduling suggestions)

**Profile Management:**
- LLM chain (profile optimization suggestions)
- Embedding chain (profile matching)

**Platform Safety:**
- Guardrails / content moderation chain

### 3. For each model document:
- Provider, model name, parameter size
- Pricing (input/output per 1M tokens)
- Latency characteristics
- Streaming support
- OpenAI API compatibility (drop-in replacement?)
- Best use case within HireLoop

### 4. Deliverable
Update the fallback system code to include ALL optimal chains per module. Update `docs/NVIDIA-NIM-HireLoop-Research.md` with the complete mapping. Ensure the /api/ai-health endpoint reflects all new providers.

## Reference
- Current fallback system: #28587
- Existing research doc: `docs/NVIDIA-NIM-HireLoop-Research.md`
- NIM catalog: https://build.nvidia.com/explore/discover
- NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY is being set in #28596

## Expected outcome
Every HireLoop module has a 3-4 deep fallback chain using the best available NIM models per modality. No module ever fully dies if one provider goes down.

### Why This Task Was Created

Owner request via chat

### Environment

Your working directory is set automatically — all Bash commands run in the repo root.
**Do NOT use `cd` to navigate.** Just run commands directly: `git log`, `cat src/app.js`, etc.

## You Are Polsia

You are part of Polsia, an autonomous AI system that builds and runs companies. You're not an assistant waiting for instructions - **you are IN CHARGE**.

### Your Mission

Build a successful business. Your job is to **IMPRESS with ACTIONS**.

Progress earns investment. Asking for help instead of delivering results loses investment.

### What You Have (Platform Capabilities)

Polsia provides EVERYTHING you need. No excuses, no blockers:

| Capability | Status | How It Works |
|------------|--------|--------------|
| **Code & Deploy** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | `polsia_infra` MCP provisions GitHub repos, Render deployments, Neon databases |
| **AI (OpenAI-compatible)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Use `new OpenAI()` with `task` field for optimal routing (see AI Proxy below) |
| **Agent SDK (Autonomous Agents)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Build AI agents that work autonomously for end users — email management, data processing, automated workflows. Agents run on Polsia infrastructure with full MCP support (Gmail, Chrome, Slack, etc). Every Polsia company can be an AI company. Engineering reads `.claude/skills/agent-sdk/SKILL.md` |
| **File Storage (R2)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | R2 proxy at `POLSIA_R2_BASE_URL` - upload images/videos for apps |
| **Twitter/X** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Posts via shared @polsiaHQ account (1 tweet/day limit) |
| **Company Email** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Every company gets `{slug}@polsia.app` - send/receive via `company_email` MCP |
| **Research** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Web search, competitive analysis |
| **Reports** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Save and query business reports |
| **Tasks** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Manage task queue and workflow |
| **Email Verification** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | `hunter_io` MCP - verify emails before cold outreach, find emails from name+domain |
| **Payments (Stripe)** | ✅ ALWAYS AVAILABLE | Polsia provides Stripe infrastructure — users do NOT connect their own Stripe account. **NEVER tell users to connect Stripe, go to Settings → Connections → Stripe, or email anyone about Stripe setup.** Create payment links via `stripe` MCP. Customer payments automatically go to company balance on Polsia. Users withdraw anytime via **Settings → Withdraw** button (Business section). 20% platform fee on withdrawals (users keep 80%). No Stripe setup required from users. |
| **Browser Automation** | ⚠️ CONDITIONAL | Browserbase + Stagehand for AI-powered browsing. QA Agent tests UIs. **BROWSE-ONLY sites (bot detection blocks login/post):** Twitter, X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, TikTok, Reddit, ProductHunt, Discord. For these sites, can only research/scrape public content - use dedicated MCPs for actions (twitter MCP for posting). For other sites (Medium, Dev.to, HackerNews, etc), create tasks with `platform` field - system handles auth. |

**If someone requests something not on this list, clarify scope before creating tasks.** Don't assume capabilities you don't have.

### Technical Stack (CRITICAL - READ THIS)

**Polsia only supports ONE application stack: Express.js + PostgreSQL**

When creating or planning infrastructure:
- **Web apps**: Use `create_instance({ template: "express-postgres" })` - this is the ONLY option
- **Template**: Express.js backend + Neon PostgreSQL database + Render deployment
- **NO other frameworks**: No Next.js, no React SPAs, no Python, no other stacks

**Why this constraint exists:**
1. Our deploy pipeline (Render) is configured for Express + Postgres
2. Start command expects `npm run migrate && npm start`
3. Database provisioning creates Neon PostgreSQL
4. All our infra tooling assumes this stack

**If a task asks for a different stack:**
- Build it with Express + Postgres instead
- The express-postgres template is flexible enough for any web app (APIs, dashboards, full-stack apps)
- Add React/Vue/etc as frontend WITHIN the Express app if needed (serve from /public or /client)

**NEVER:**
- Create a Next.js app
- Create a standalone React/Vue/Angular SPA
- Use Python, Ruby, Go, or other backends
- Use any database other than PostgreSQL

### How to Think About "Blockers"

❌ WRONG: "We're blocked on OAuth. Waiting for someone."
✅ RIGHT: "Platform provides everything we need. Building now."

❌ WRONG: "No one has responded to our request."
✅ RIGHT: "Proceeding with our plan."

❌ WRONG: "We need approval before making changes."
✅ RIGHT: "Making this change. Will report what we did."

### Your Culture

Act first, report later. Ship progress every day—no excuses, no waiting.

### The Owner Relationship

The owner is like an investor who pays the bills:
- **Active investors** provide feedback and guidance
- **Passive investors** just pay and watch
- Either way, **your job is to IMPRESS with ACTIONS**

- Listen to their feedback — they're paying
- But you make the decisions — you're the operator
- They might not respond → **proceed anyway**
- Ask for approval on major pivots (vision/goals changes)
- Never ask for help with execution → just do it

**If the owner doesn't respond, that's NORMAL.** Proceed with your plan.

### When to Actually Ask the Owner

Only escalate when:
- Major pivot to vision or goals
- Legal/compliance decisions
- Unclear business direction after several days

Everything else? Just do it and report what you did.

### Polsia Business Model (Answer User Questions)

**Simple Pricing:**
- **Base Plan**: $29/mo - 1 company, daily progress, 15 instant tasks/month
- **+Company Add-on**: +$29/mo each - adds 1 company with daily progress
- **+Task Pack Add-on**: +$29/mo each - adds 30 instant tasks/month

**Instant Tasks:**
- Owners can run individual tasks immediately (instead of waiting for the daily cycle)
- Credits reset monthly with subscription renewal
- More packs = faster progress

**Referral Program:**
- Share referral link from the menu → "Refer a Friend"

## Task Guidelines

### Before Creating Tasks

Check the task backlog. Don't create duplicates.

- Similar task exists and not done → skip
- Similar task completed recently → skip unless it failed
- Similar task rejected → skip unless conditions changed

### Creating Tasks

Create 1-2 tasks per cycle max. Pick the highest-impact work.

Use `create_task_proposal()` for work another agent should do:
- Engineering work → Engineering agent

Use `capabilities.find_agent_for_task()` to find the right agent.

Set `complexity` 1-10. Higher = better model. Bump up for hard bugs, failed attempts, or frustrated users.

### Owner-Created Tasks

If a task has `source: owner_request`, prioritize it. The owner is paying.

### No Blockers

You don't wait for approval. You don't ask for permission.
If you're unsure, make the call and move on.

### Report Platform Bugs

If a tool behaves unexpectedly, report it via `polsia_support.report_platform_bug()`.
Don't work around it silently — the platform team needs to know.

## POLSIA INFRASTRUCTURE ISSUES (CRITICAL)

You are working in a POLSIA setup. You have a team that gave you tools, orchestrations, and subagents - but they might be inefficient or incomplete. When you encounter infrastructure issues, **NEVER ask the customer to fix them** - customers don't have access to Render, API keys, databases, or platform config.

### WHAT TO DO WHEN BLOCKED

1. **Report it to Polsia** using `polsia_support` MCP tools
2. **Move on** - explain why you couldn't complete that part
3. **Tell the user** to contact support@polsia.com for urgent issues (billing, credits, refunds, account access)

### REPORT THESE ISSUES (use report_platform_bug OR suggest_feature):

- **API Keys**: Missing, invalid, or inaccessible API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, any external service)
- **Render/Deploy**: Can't push, deploy fails, service unreachable, env vars not set
- **Database**: Connection failures, missing DATABASE_URL, migration issues
- **Env Variables**: Any env var that should be set but isn't (in dev or prod)
- **Testing Issues**: Can't run tests because of missing config/credentials
- **MCP Failures**: Tools not available, servers not mounting, unexpected errors
- **Missing Capabilities**: Customer wants a feature that requires a service/API we haven't integrated yet
- **Subagent Issues**: Better coordination, new subagents needed, workflow improvements
- **New Tools/APIs**: Any tool or integration that would help you do your job better

### EXAMPLE RESPONSES

**WRONG** (never do this):
> "Please check your Render dashboard and verify the DATABASE_URL is set correctly."

**RIGHT** (do this instead):
> "I encountered a DATABASE_URL configuration issue. I've reported this to the Polsia team (report_platform_bug). Moving on to the parts I can complete. This will be fixed soon."

### KEY MINDSET

You're part of a team building autonomous infrastructure. If something is broken or missing, report it - **Polsia will build it**. Your job is to:
1. Do what you can
2. Report what's blocking you
3. Move on and explain what was skipped
4. Never leave the customer hanging or ask them to do ops work

# NVIDIA NIM Complete Model Catalog — All Providers

**Date:** February 11, 2026
**Replaces:** Report #16624 (which only covered NVIDIA-branded models)
**Purpose:** Comprehensive catalog of EVERY model on [build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com/models) with module mapping for Rekrut AI (HireLoop)

---

## Executive Summary

- **210+ model endpoints** across **25+ providers** on NVIDIA NIM — not just NVIDIA models
- **Kimi K2.5 IS available** on NIM — 1T parameter multimodal MoE with 256K context, free prototyping
- **GPT-OSS-120B and GPT-OSS-20B** (OpenAI's open-weight models) are on NIM — Apache 2.0 licensed
- **Best value for HireLoop: 4-model strategy** using Nemotron Super 49B, DeepSeek V3.1, Kimi K2.5, and NV-EmbedQA — projected **90%+ cost savings** vs GPT-4o
- **NIM pricing is credit-based for prototyping** (1,000 free credits), GPU-licensed for production ($4,500/GPU/year), or per-token via third-party providers like DeepInfra

---

## 1. All Providers on NVIDIA NIM

| # | Provider | Models on NIM | Specialization |
|---|----------|--------------|----------------|
| 1 | **NVIDIA** | 30+ | Nemotron LLMs, Parakeet ASR, Cosmos VLMs, NemoGuard safety, NeMo Retriever, Maxine audio, Nemotron-Parse OCR |

Here are the search results for **NVIDIA NIM Riva TTS API endpoint format** for text-to-speech:

## Overview

Riva TTS NIM APIs provide easy access to state-of-the-art text-to-speech (TTS) models, capable of synthesizing English speech from text with exceptional accuracy. Riva TTS NIM models are built on the NVIDIA software platform, incorporating CUDA, TensorRT, and Triton to offer out-of-the-box performance.

---

## API Endpoints

### 1. **HTTP Endpoint: `/v1/audio/synthesize`**

The primary HTTP endpoint for TTS synthesis uses form-data parameters. For example:

```bash
curl -sS http://localhost:9000/v1/audio/synthesize --fail-with-body \
  -F language=en-US \
  -F text="Your text here" \
  -F voice=Magpie-Multilingual.EN-US.Aria \
  --output output.wav
```

The documentation demonstrates various TTS models using gRPC and HTTP APIs using sample Python clients and curl commands respectively.

### 2. **HTTP Endpoint: `/v1/audio/list_voices`**

You can query the available voices using the `/v1/audio/list_voices` HTTP endpoint. The response includes voices organized by language (e.g., `en-US`, `es-ES`), and available voices and models depend on your Riva deployment configuration. Use the `list_voices` endpoint to discover what's available in your environment.

### 3. **gRPC Endpoint (Port 50051)**

Riva TTS supports a number of options when making a text-to-speech request to the gRPC endpoint, including: `language_code` (e.g., `en-US`), `encoding` (`LINEAR_PCM` and `OGGOPUS` supported), `sample_rate_hz` (usually 22kHz or 44kHz), and `voice_name`.

### 4. **Realtime WebSocket API (Port 9000)**

The realtime server uses a WebSocket-based event system for communication between clients and the server. Events are JSON messages that follow a specific format and are used to handle various operations like session management, text processing, and speech synthesis.

---

## Streaming vs. Offline Modes

- In offline mode, audio is not returned until the full audio sequence for the requested text is generated and can achieve higher throughput.
- When making a streaming request, audio chunks are returned as soon as they are generated, significantly reducing the latency (as measured by time to first audio) for large requests.
- The Offline API returns synthesized speech in a single chunk, and an error will occur if the synthesized speech exceeds the 4 MB gRPC limit. In such cases, using the Streaming API is recommended.

---

## Voice Cloning (Zero Shot)

The `zero_shot_config` allows for voice cloning using audio prompts. When provided, the system will attempt to synthesize speech in a voice similar to the provided audio prompt. The `audio_prompt_bytes` should be base64-encoded audio data.

Requirements for audio prompts:
- Audio format must be 16-bit Mono WAV file with a sample rate of 22.05 kHz or higher. Aim for a duration of five seconds. Trim silence from the beginning and end so that speech fills most of the prompt. Record the prompt in a noise-free environment.

---

## Supported Models

The TTS pipeline is based on a two-stage pipeline. Riva models like FastPitch and RadTTS++ first generate a mel-spectrogram and then generate speech using the HifiGAN model, while MagpieTTS Multilingual generates tokens and then generates speech using the Audio Codec model.

The Magpie TTS Multilingual model supports text-to-speech in multiple languages.

---

## Key Resources

- **Official Docs**: [Riva TTS NIM Overview](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/riva/tts/latest/overview.html)
- **Realtime API Reference**: [Realtime API](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/riva/tts/latest/realtime-tts.html)
- **gRPC API Reference**: [gRPC Protos](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/riva/tts/latest/protos.html)
- **Getting Started**: [Getting Started Guide](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/riva/tts/latest/getting-started.html)
- **NGC Container**: [NVIDIA NGC Riva TTS](https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nim/teams/nvidia/containers/riva-tts)

Here's a comprehensive summary of what's available regarding **NVIDIA NIM Speech Models API**, including Parakeet, Riva, TTS, ASR, and endpoint formats:

---

## NVIDIA NIM Speech Models — Parakeet, Riva, TTS & ASR

### Overview

NVIDIA NIM, part of NVIDIA AI Enterprise, provides containers to self-host GPU-accelerated inferencing microservices for pretrained and customized AI models. The speech and translation microservices leverage NVIDIA Riva and provide automatic speech recognition (ASR), neural machine translation (NMT), and text-to-speech (TTS) services.

### Parakeet ASR Models

The Parakeet family of automatic speech recognition (ASR) models, developed by NVIDIA in collaboration with Suno.ai, offers state-of-the-art accuracy in transcribing spoken English. The models, available in 0.6B and 1.1B parameter sizes, are trained on a 64,000-hour dataset and demonstrate exceptional accuracy across diverse accents, dialects, and noise conditions.

Key Parakeet models available through NIM include:

- To use the Parakeet 0.6b CTC English model, set `CONTAINER_ID` to `parakeet-0-6b-ctc-en-us`.
- To use the Parakeet 1.1b CTC English model, set `CONTAINER_ID` to `parakeet-1-1b-ctc-en-us`.
- Parakeet 0.6b TDT v2 is a 600-million-parameter ASR model designed for high-quality English transcription, featuring support for punctuation, capitalization, and accurate timestamp prediction.
- Parakeet 1.1b RNNT Multilingual model supports streaming speech-to-text transcription in multiple languages, and the model identifies the spoken language and provides the transcript corresponding to the spoken language.

### Endpoint Format & API

The NIM containers expose both **HTTP** and **gRPC** endpoints:

The container runs with `NIM_HTTP_API_PORT=9000` and `NIM_GRPC_API_PORT=50051`, mapped to ports 9000 and 50051 respectively.

- The ASR HTTP API (Whisper-compatible) accepts only `file`, `model`, and `language` parameters in the request. All other parameters are ignored.
- It supports only the offline Recognize API.
- Support has been added for Realtime API via WebSockets for streaming ASR applications.

### Model Format & Profiles

By default, the NIM container downloads ONNX-formatted model files that run on any sufficiently advanced NVIDIA GPU. However, if you change the `NIM_MANIFEST_PROFILE` parameter appropriately, you'll instead download NVIDIA TensorRT-formatted model files optimized for H100, A100, or L40 GPUs.

The environment variable `NIM_TAGS_SELECTOR` is used to specify the desired model and inference mode, specified as comma-separated key-value pairs.

### TTS Support

Magpie-TTS has been added for Jetson platforms, a multilingual TTS model supporting English, Spanish, French, Italian, German, Vietnamese, and Mandarin, with high-quality Male and Female emotion subvoices. Note: SSL/TLS encryption does not support TTS APIs currently.

### Recent Updates (Release Notes)

- The Parakeet CTC 1.1b English (En-US) model has been updated with merged default and telephony profiles, along with improved accuracy.
- Support was added for the DGX Spark platform for the Parakeet CTC 1.1b English and Parakeet 1.1b RNNT Multilingual models, and Blackwell GPU support was added for the Parakeet 1.1b RNNT Multilingual.
- Sortformer support was added to the Parakeet 1.1b RNNT Multilingual model.

### Deployment on Cloud (e.g., AWS)

The NIM container enables a comprehensive audio processing pipeline that seamlessly combines speech recognition with speaker identification through the NVIDIA Riva integrated capabilities. The output includes the full transcription, timestamped segments with speaker attribution, confidence scores, and total speaker count in a structured JSON format.

### NeMo Framework Integration

NeMo ASR and TTS models can be optimized for inference and deployed for production use cases with NVIDIA Riva. If using NVIDIA NeMo, first convert the model from `.nemo` format to `.riva` format using the `nemo2riva` tool, then use the Riva ASR NIM container and tools (`riva-build` and `riva-deploy`) for deployment.

---

For the most up-to-date details, you can explore models directly at [NVIDIA NIM Speech API Catalog](https://build.nvidia.com/explore/speech) and the official [NVIDIA NIM Riva ASR documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/riva/asr/latest/).

## NVIDIA NIM TTS — Overview

Riva TTS NIM APIs provide easy access to state-of-the-art text-to-speech (TTS) models, capable of synthesizing English speech from text with exceptional accuracy. Riva TTS NIM models are built on the NVIDIA software platform, incorporating CUDA, TensorRT, and Triton to offer out-of-the-box GPU acceleration.

Riva TTS NIM comes with enterprise-ready features, such as a high-performance inference server, flexible integration, and enterprise-grade security. Additionally, it is built on NVIDIA NeMo, allowing for seamless integration and customization, with ready-to-use pre-trained checkpoints for inference or fine-tuning, released under a CC-BY-4.0 license for commercial use.

---

## Available TTS Models on NVIDIA NIM

NIM microservices for speech and translation are now available. The new speech and translation microservices leverage NVIDIA Riva and provide automatic speech recognition (ASR), neural machine translation (NMT), and text-to-speech (TTS) services.

Key hosted TTS models include:

1. **Magpie TTS Multilingual** — Available as a multilingual TTS Text-to-Speech model via NVIDIA NIM and NVIDIA Riva. The Magpie TTS Flow model supports text-to-speech in English using an audio prompt and prompt transcript text, with voice characteristics from the audio prompt applied to the synthesized output speech. Compared to the Magpie TTS Zeroshot model, this model requires additional prompt transcript text as input.

2. **RadTTS-HifiGAN TTS** — Available on `build.nvidia.com`, accessible via a gRPC client pointing to `grpc.nvcf.nvidia.com:443` with a specific function ID and API key.

3. **Fastpitch HifiGAN TTS** — The Fastpitch HifiGAN TTS model supports text-to-speech only in English (en-US) language.

4. **Nemotron Speech** — Capable of converting written text to spoken audio in multiple languages, with natural and expressive voices designed for voice agents and brand ambassadors.

---

## Cloud-Hosted vs. Self-Hosted Options

You can get access to unlimited prototyping with hosted APIs for NIM accelerated by DGX Cloud, or download and self-host NIM microservices. NVIDIA NIM combines the ease of use and operational simplicity of managed APIs with the flexibility and security of self-hosting models on your preferred infrastructure.

You can seamlessly transition from cloud endpoints to self-hosted APIs without code changes. Start with free access for development and testing, and move to an NVIDIA AI Enterprise license for production.

---

## Realtime TTS API

Riva Realtime Server provides a WebSocket-based API for real-time text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis. This API allows you to stream text data and receive real-time speech audio output. The WebSocket server provides real-time communication capabilities for text-to-speech synthesis services.

The zero_shot_config allows for voice cloning using audio prompts. When provided, the system will attempt to synthesize speech in a voice similar to the provided audio prompt. The audio_prompt_bytes should be base64-encoded audio data.

---

## Licensing

Riva TTS NIM is available for self-hosting under the NVIDIA AI Enterprise (NVAIE) License. Riva TTS NIM runs on any NVIDIA GPU with sufficient GPU memory, but some model/GPU combinations are optimized.

   177→      this.nimBaseUrl = nimBase;
   178→      this.nimApiKey = nimKey;
   179→      this.nimAvailable = true;

   311→  // Check if a provider is available (has required client/keys)

   316→      case 'openai':

   320→      case 'openai_embed':
   321→        return !!this.clients.openai;
   322→      // All NIM providers require NIM API key
   323→      case 'nim_nemotron_super':
   324→      case 'nim_kimi':
   325→      case 'nim_deepseek_v3':
   326→      case 'nim_step_flash':
   327→      case 'nim_gpt_oss':
   328→      case 'nim_nano':
   329→      case 'nim_mistral_small':
   330→      case 'nim_cosmos':
   331→      case 'nim_nemotron_vl':
   332→      case 'nim_kimi_vision':
   333→      case 'nim_riva_tts':
   334→      case 'nim_parakeet_v2':
   335→      case 'nim_parakeet_v3':
   336→      case 'nim_embed_qa':

   347→
   348→  // ─── Circuit Breaker ──────────────────────────────────────────
   349→
   350→  isCircuitOpen(providerKey, modality) {
   351→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   352→    const failure = this.failures[key];
   353→    if (!failure) return false;
   354→
   355→    const elapsed = Date.now() - failure.lastFailure;
   356→    if (elapsed > CIRCUIT_BREAK_MS) {
   357→      // Circuit closed — enough time passed, allow retry
   358→      delete this.failures[key];
   359→      return false;
   360→    }
   361→    return true;
   362→  }
   363→
   364→  recordFailure(providerKey, modality, error) {
   365→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   366→    const existing = this.failures[key] || { count: 0 };
   367→    const status = error.status || error.statusCode || null;
   368→
   369→    this.failures[key] = {
   370→      count: existing.count + 1,
   371→      lastFailure: Date.now(),
   372→      error: error.message || String(error),
   373→      status,
   374→    };
   375→
   376→    this.stats.totalFailovers++;
   377→
   378→    const logEntry = {
   379→      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   380→      event: 'provider_failure',
   381→      provider: providerKey,
   382→      modality,
   383→      error: error.message || String(error),
   384→      status,
   385→    };
   386→    this.logs.push(logEntry);
   387→    if (this.logs.length > 100) this.logs.shift();
   388→
   389→    console.log(`[ai-provider] ❌ ${providerKey} failed for ${modality}: [${status || 'ERR'}] ${error.message || error}`);
   390→  }
   391→
   392→  recordSuccess(providerKey, modality) {
   393→    this.activeProviders[modality] = providerKey;
   394→    const key = `${providerKey}:${modality}`;
   395→    delete this.failures[key];
   396→
   397→    this.stats.totalCalls++;
   398→    this.stats.providerCalls[providerKey] = (this.stats.providerCalls[providerKey] || 0) + 1;
   399→  }
   400→
   401→  isRetryable(err) {
   402→    // Network/timeout errors
   403→    if (err.code === 'ETIMEDOUT' || err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED' || err.code === 'ECONNRESET' || err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') return true;
   404→    if (err.message && (err.message.includes('timeout') || err.message.includes('ETIMEDOUT'))) return true;
   405→
   406→    // HTTP status codes
   407→    const status = err.status || err.statusCode || (err.response && err.response.status);
   408→    if (status && RETRYABLE_STATUS.has(status)) return true;
   409→
   410→    // OpenAI SDK-specific error types
   411→    if (err.type === 'insufficient_quota' || err.code === 'insufficient_quota') return true;
   412→    if (err.type === 'rate_limit_exceeded' || err.code === 'rate_limit_exceeded') return true;
   413→
   414→    // Anthropic-specific
   415→    if (err.error && err.error.type === 'overloaded_error') return true;
   416→
   417→    return false;
   418→  }
   419→
   420→  // Get available providers for a modality (skipping unavailable + circuit-broken)
   421→  getAvailableChain(modality, variant = 'default') {
   422→    return this.getChain(modality, variant).filter(key =>
   423→      this.isProviderAvailable(key) && !this.isCircuitOpen(key, modality)
   424→    );
   425→  }
   426→
   427→  // ─── Core Execution with Fallback ────────────────────────────
   428→
   429→  /**
   430→   * Execute an AI call with automatic fallback.
   431→   * @param {string} modality - 'llm', 'vision', 'tts', 'asr', 'embedding', 'reranking', 'safety'
   432→   * @param {Function} executeFn - async (providerKey) => result
   433→   * @param {string} variant - Chain variant (default, quality, efficient, reasoning)
   434→   * @returns {*} Result from the first successful provider
   435→   */
   436→  async execute(modality, executeFn, variant = 'default') {
   437→    const chain = this.getAvailableChain(modality, variant);
   438→
   439→    if (chain.length === 0) {
   440→      const allChain = this.getChain(modality, variant);
   441→      const reasons = allChain.map(k => {
   442→        if (!this.isProviderAvailable(k)) return `${k}: not configured`;
   443→        if (this.isCircuitOpen(k, modality)) return `${k}: circuit open`;
   444→        return `${k}: unknown`;
   445→      });
   446→      throw new Error(`No available providers for ${modality}. ${reasons.join(', ')}`);
   447→    }
   448→
   449→    let lastError;
   450→    for (const providerKey of chain) {
   451→      try {
   452→        const result = await executeFn(providerKey);
   453→        this.recordSuccess(providerKey, modality);
   454→        return result;
   455→      } catch (err) {
   456→        lastError = err;
   457→        if (this.isRetryable(err)) {
   458→          this.recordFailure(providerKey, modality, err);
   459→          continue; // try next provider
   460→        }
   461→        // Non-retryable error (e.g., bad request, invalid input) — don't try others
   462→        throw err;
   463→      }
   464→    }
   465→
   466→    // All providers exhausted
   467→    console.error(`[ai-provider] All ${modality} providers exhausted.`);
   468→    throw lastError || new Error(`All ${modality} providers failed`);
   469→  }

   474→   * Chat completion with automatic provider fallback.

   510→  async _anthropicChat(messages, options) {
   511→    const systemMsgs = messages.filter(m => m.role === 'system');
   512→    const nonSystemMsgs = messages.filter(m => m.role !== 'system');
   513→
   514→    const systemPrompt = options.system || (systemMsgs.length > 0 ? systemMsgs[0].content : undefined);
   515→    const msgs = nonSystemMsgs.map(m => ({
   516→      role: m.role,
   517→      content: typeof m.content === 'string' ? m.content : String(m.content),
   518→    }));
   519→    if (msgs.length === 0) msgs.push({ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' });
   520→
   521→    const response = await this.clients.anthropic.messages.create({
   522→      max_tokens: options.maxTokens || options.max_tokens || 8192,
   523→      messages: msgs,
   524→      system: systemPrompt,
   525→    }, {
   526→      headers: options.subscriptionId ? { 'X-Subscription-ID': options.subscriptionId } : {},
   527→    });
   528→
   529→    return response.content[0].text;
   530→  }

   532→  async _openaiChat(model, messages, options, isPolsia = false) {
   533→    const client = this.clients.openai;
   534→    const msgs = [];
   535→
   536→    if (options.system) {
   537→      msgs.push({ role: 'system', content: options.system });
   538→    }
   539→    for (const m of messages) {
   540→      if (m.role === 'system' && options.system) continue; // already added
   541→      msgs.push({ role: m.role, content: typeof m.content === 'string' ? m.content : String(m.content) });
   542→    }
   543→    if (msgs.length === 0) msgs.push({ role: 'user', content: 'Hello' });
   544→
   545→    const params = {
   546→      model,
   547→      messages: msgs,
   548→      max_tokens: options.maxTokens || options.max_tokens || 8192,
   549→      temperature: options.temperature || 0.7,
   550→    };
   551→
   552→    // Polsia proxy requires task field
   553→    if (isPolsia) params.task = 'chat-fallback';
   554→    if (options.response_format) params.response_format = options.response_format;
   555→
   556→    const response = await client.chat.completions.create(params);
   557→    return response.choices[0].message.content;
   558→  }

   650→  // ─── Text-to-Speech ──────────────────────────────────────────
   651→
   652→  /**
   653→   * Text-to-speech with automatic provider fallback.
   654→   * Chain: OpenAI TTS → NIM Riva TTS → null (browser Web Speech API)
   655→   * @param {string} text - Text to convert to speech
   656→   * @param {Object} options - { voice, speed, subscriptionId }
   657→   * @returns {Buffer|null} Audio buffer (MP3/WAV) or null if all providers fail
   658→   */
   659→  async textToSpeech(text, options = {}) {
   660→    try {
   661→      return await this.execute('tts', async (providerKey) => {
   662→        switch (providerKey) {
   663→          case 'openai_tts':
   664→            return this._openaiTTS(text, options);
   665→          case 'nim_riva_tts':
   666→            return this._nimRivaTTS(text, options);
   667→          default:
   668→            throw new Error(`Unknown TTS provider: ${providerKey}`);
   669→        }
   670→      });
   671→    } catch (err) {
   672→      // TTS has browser fallback — return null to signal frontend to use Web Speech API
   673→      console.error('[ai-provider] All TTS providers failed, frontend will use browser fallback:', err.message);
   674→      return null;
   675→    }
   676→  }
   677→
   678→  async _openaiTTS(text, options) {
   679→    const voice = options.voice || 'nova';
   680→    const speed = options.speed || 1.0;
   681→    const truncatedText = text.length > 4000 ? text.substring(0, 4000) : text;
   682→
   683→    const baseUrl = this.openaiBaseUrl || 'https://api.openai.com/v1';
   684→    const apiKey = this.openaiApiKey;
   685→
   686→    const headers = {
   687→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
   688→      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   689→    };
   690→    if (options.subscriptionId) headers['X-Subscription-ID'] = options.subscriptionId;
   691→
   692→    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/audio/speech`, {
   693→      method: 'POST',
   694→      headers,
   695→      body: JSON.stringify({
   696→        model: 'tts-1',
   697→        input: truncatedText,
   698→        voice,
   699→        response_format: 'mp3',
   700→        speed,
   701→        task: 'tts-interview',
   702→      }),
   703→    });
   704→
   705→    if (!res.ok) {
   706→      const errText = await res.text();
   707→      const err = new Error(`TTS API error: ${res.status} ${errText.substring(0, 200)}`);
   708→      err.status = res.status;
   709→      throw err;
   710→    }
   711→

## 13. Recommended Architecture: 5-Model Strategy

Previous research recommended 3 models. With the full catalog visible, **5 models** covers everything:

| Role | Model | Provider | Monthly Cost Est. (10K interactions) |
|------|-------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| **Primary LLM** | Nemotron Super 49B v1.5 | NVIDIA | ~$15-40/mo |
| **Bulk/Simple Tasks** | Nemotron Nano 9B v2 | NVIDIA | ~$5-15/mo |
| **Embeddings + Matching** | NV-EmbedQA-1B-v2 + NV-RerankQA-1B-v2 | NVIDIA | ~$2-5/mo |
| **Document Parsing** | Nemotron-Parse | NVIDIA | ~$1-3/mo |
| **Voice/ASR** | Parakeet-TDT-0.6B v2 | NVIDIA | ~$1-2/mo |

**Estimated total: $24-65/month** vs **$775-2,210/month with GPT-4o** = **90-97% savings**

### When to Use Non-NVIDIA Models

| Scenario | Use Instead | Why |
|----------|------------|-----|
| Need 256K+ context for long documents | **Kimi K2.5** or **Step 3.5 Flash** | 256K context vs 128K on Nemotron |
| Need video analysis in interviews | **Kimi K2.5** | Native video understanding |
| Need ultra-cheap reasoning | **DeepSeek R1 Distill 70B** | $0.03/1M input tokens |
| Need multilingual (100+ languages) | **Qwen3-Next-80B** | 119 language support |
| Need advanced coding/automation | **Qwen3-Coder-480B** | Best coding benchmarks |
| Need reasoning for math/logic tests | **Step 3.5 Flash** | 97.3% AIME, 350 tok/s |
| Free prototyping of frontier models | **GPT-OSS-120B** or **Kimi K2.5** | Free on NIM trial |

---

## 14. API Compatibility

**ALL models on NIM use the OpenAI-compatible API format.** Migration from GPT-4o requires only changing:

```
base_url: "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1"
model: "nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5"  // or any other model
```

No code rewrite needed. Same `chat/completions` endpoint, same request/response format.

---

## 15. Key Differences from Previous Research (Report #16624)

| What Changed | Previous Report | This Report |
|-------------|----------------|-------------|
| **Providers covered** | NVIDIA only (Nemotron, Parakeet) | 25+ providers including Meta, DeepSeek, Moonshot, OpenAI, Qwen, etc. |
| **Total models** | 82 (mostly NVIDIA) | **210+ endpoints** across all providers |
| **Kimi K2.5** | Not mentioned | ✅ Fully cataloged — 1T param multimodal, available on NIM |
| **GPT-OSS** | Not mentioned | ✅ OpenAI's open models on NIM, Apache 2.0 |
| **Step 3.5 Flash** | Not mentioned | ✅ 97.3% AIME, best efficiency per parameter |
| **DeepSeek V3.1/V3.2** | Not mentioned | ✅ GPT-5 competitive, $0.28/1M tokens |
| **Strategy** | 3-model (all NVIDIA) | **5-model primary + situational non-NVIDIA models** |
| **Coding models** | None | Qwen3-Coder, Devstral, Code Llama, StarCoder |
| **Image generation** | None | Stable Diffusion 3.5, FLUX.1, Trellis |

---

- [NVIDIA NIM Model Catalog](https://build.nvidia.com/models)
- [NVIDIA NIM Supported LLMs](https://docs.nvidia.com/nim/large-language-models/latest/supported-models.html)

## 13. Comprehensive Fallback Chains Per HireLoop Module (IMPLEMENTED)

**Updated:** February 11, 2026 — All chains are now live in `lib/ai-provider.js`

Every HireLoop module has a 3-7 deep fallback chain. No module ever fully dies if one provider goes down. Chains are variant-based: `quality` (best models first), `efficient` (cheapest first), `reasoning` (logic-optimized), or `default` (balanced).

### 13.1 LLM Fallback Chains

| Variant | Chain (in order) | Use Case |
|---------|-----------------|----------|
| **default** | Anthropic → OpenAI → Nemotron Super 49B → Kimi K2.5 → DeepSeek V3.2 → Step 3.5 Flash → GPT-OSS-120B | General purpose |
| **quality** | Anthropic → OpenAI → DeepSeek V3.2 → Kimi K2.5 → Nemotron Super 49B → Step 3.5 Flash → GPT-OSS-120B | Mock Interview, Coaching |
| **efficient** | Anthropic → OpenAI → Nemotron Nano 9B → Mistral Small 24B → Nemotron Super 49B → GPT-OSS-120B | Onboarding, Payroll, Offers, Scheduling |
| **reasoning** | Anthropic → OpenAI → Step 3.5 Flash → DeepSeek V3.2 → Kimi K2.5 → Nemotron Super 49B | Assessments |

### 13.2 Vision Fallback Chains

| Variant | Chain (in order) | Use Case |
|---------|-----------------|----------|
| **default** | OpenAI GPT-4o → Cosmos Reason2 8B → Nemotron Nano 12B VL → Kimi K2.5 | Body language analysis |
| **document** | OpenAI GPT-4o → Nemotron Nano 12B VL → Kimi K2.5 → Cosmos Reason2 8B | Resume parsing/OCR |

### 13.3 TTS Fallback Chain

| Position | Provider | Details |
|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | **OpenAI TTS-1** (Polsia proxy) | Primary — `nova` voice, MP3 output |
| 2 | **NIM Riva TTS** | NVIDIA speech synthesis via `/v1/audio/synthesize` |
| 3 | **Browser Web Speech API** | Final fallback at frontend level (no server call) |

### 13.4 ASR Fallback Chain

| Position | Provider | Details |
|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | **OpenAI Whisper** (Polsia proxy) | Primary — `whisper-1`, verbose_json output |
| 2 | **NIM Parakeet TDT 0.6B v2** | English #1 on HF leaderboard, 6.05% WER |
| 3 | **NIM Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3** | 25 European languages, auto language detect |

### 13.5 Embedding Fallback Chain

| Position | Provider | Details |
|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | **OpenAI text-embedding-3-small** (Polsia proxy) | Primary — 1536 dimensions |
| 2 | **NIM NV-EmbedQA-1B-v2** | 26 languages, Matryoshka embeddings |
| 3 | **NIM Nemotron-Embed-VL-1B-v2** | Multimodal (text + image) embeddings |

### 13.6 Reranking Chain (NEW)

| Position | Provider | Details |
|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | **NIM NV-RerankQA-1B-v2** | Text document reranking for RAG |
| 2 | **NIM Nemotron-Rerank-VL-1B-v2** | Multimodal reranking |

### 13.7 Safety/Guardrails Chain (NEW)

| Position | Provider | Details |
|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | **NIM NemoGuard Safety 8B** | 23 safety categories, multilingual |
| 2 | **NIM Nemotron Content Safety 4B** | Domain-specific safety policies |

### 13.8 Module → Chain Mapping

| HireLoop Module | LLM | TTS | ASR | Vision | Embedding | Reranking | Safety |
|----------------|-----|-----|-----|--------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| **Mock Interview** | quality (7) | default (2) | default (3) | default (4) | — | — | — |
| **AI Coaching** | quality (7) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Resume Parsing** | default (7) | — | — | document (4) | — | — | — |
| **Job Matching** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | default (3) | default (2) | — |
| **Onboarding** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Assessments** | reasoning (6) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Offer Management** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Payroll** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Scheduling** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Profile Management** | efficient (6) | — | — | — | default (3) | — | — |
| **Platform Safety** | default (7) | — | — | — | — | — | default (2) |

*Numbers in parentheses = chain depth (providers available before total failure)*

---

## 15. Architecture: Comprehensive Fallback System

### Total Provider Depth Per Modality

| Modality | Polsia Providers | NIM Providers | Total Depth | Browser Fallback |
|----------|-----------------|---------------|-------------|-----------------|
| **LLM** | 2 (Anthropic + OpenAI) | 5 (Nemotron, Kimi, DeepSeek, Step, GPT-OSS) | **7** | — |
| **Vision** | 1 (GPT-4o) | 3 (Cosmos, Nemotron VL, Kimi) | **4** | — |
| **TTS** | 1 (OpenAI TTS-1) | 1 (Riva TTS) | **2** + browser | ✅ Web Speech API |
| **ASR** | 1 (Whisper) | 2 (Parakeet v2, v3) | **3** | — |
| **Embedding** | 1 (text-embedding-3-small) | 2 (EmbedQA, Embed-VL) | **3** | — |
| **Reranking** | 0 | 2 (RerankQA, Rerank-VL) | **2** | Graceful degrade |
| **Safety** | 0 | 2 (NemoGuard, Content Safety) | **2** | Allow-by-default |

### What Changed from Previous System (#28587)

| Aspect | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Total NIM models registered** | 7 | **22** |
| **LLM chain depth** | 5 | **7** (with variants) |
| **TTS chain depth** | 1 + browser | **2 + browser** (added Riva TTS) |
| **ASR chain depth** | 2 | **3** (added Parakeet v3) |
| **Vision chain depth** | 3 | **4** (added Kimi K2.5 multimodal) |
| **Embedding chain depth** | 2 | **3** (added Nemotron-Embed-VL) |
| **New: Reranking** | None | **2 providers** |
| **New: Safety** | None | **2 providers** |
| **Per-module routing** | None | **11 modules** with variant chains |
| **Matching engine fallback** | Direct OpenAI only | **Full 3-provider chain** |

### Estimated Monthly Cost (NIM-primary, 10K interactions)

| Role | Model | Cost |
|------|-------|------|
| Primary LLM | Nemotron Super 49B | ~$15-40/mo |
| Bulk Tasks | Nemotron Nano 9B | ~$5-15/mo |
| Embeddings | NV-EmbedQA-1B | ~$2-5/mo |
| Reranking | NV-RerankQA-1B | ~$1-2/mo |
| ASR | Parakeet TDT 0.6B | ~$1-2/mo |
| TTS | Riva TTS | ~$2-5/mo |
| Safety | NemoGuard 8B | ~$1-2/mo |
| **Total** | | **$27-71/mo** |

vs **$775-2,210/mo with GPT-4o** = **90-97% savings**

---

## 16. API Compatibility

**ALL LLM/Vision/Embedding models on NIM use the OpenAI-compatible API format.** Migration requires only changing:

```
base_url: "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1"
model: "nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5"
```

**Exceptions** (different API formats):
- **TTS (Riva)**: Uses `/v1/audio/synthesize` with form-data (NOT `/v1/audio/speech`)
- **Reranking**: Uses `/v1/ranking` with query/passages JSON format
- **Safety**: Uses chat/completions format with safety-specific system prompts

## 13. Comprehensive Fallback Chains Per HireLoop Module (IMPLEMENTED)

**Updated:** February 11, 2026 — All chains are now live in `lib/ai-provider.js`

Every HireLoop module has a 3-7 deep fallback chain. No module ever fully dies if one provider goes down. Chains are variant-based: `quality` (best models first), `efficient` (cheapest first), `reasoning` (logic-optimized), or `default` (balanced).
